reviews.review_description: As far as puzzle games go this is absolutely fine, there's plenty to go at and the ability to make your own can add hours more entertainment. Puzzle pieces can be a little tricky at times and if you're doing a big puzzle the music could do with shuffling between multiple tracks or just some ambiant sounds as you start to notice the loops a bit. If you want a puzzle game I would find it hard to talk you out of this one

That's the summary for now, I can't give more than 2 stars to the game right now, and it has some issues. It feels very in progress. For the devs:

First,the scale of things is too big for a jigsaw puzzle. It gets unwieldy, the pieces are cumbersome to rotate around, they obstruct your view around, it makes the player need more space to play, etc. Reduce the general scale a 30% at least. Bonus points if you get to be player customizable (it would be cool if you could zoom in/out).
Second, there is no reference picture/model in your 3d jigsaw puzzle game. That's... Puzzling, no pun intended.
Third, you have put annoying music in the game and no options menu to turn it off.
Fourth, there are no cursor or force-grab action to pick far away pieces.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to leave a review! You probably tried one of the giant puzzles, maybe try a smaller one, like "Avocado, Sliced in Two"?
The puzzles are varying in size, for diversity, a few are giant (like the Perseverance Rover, at full scale! Other players told us this model was quite fun for them, if a bit absurd!) but most puzzles are much smaller. You are100% right that we could add an indication of the size in later updates.
Absence of reference: it's a choice we made, to let players discover the model as a surprise. It has been appreciated by other players. If you really want to know the model before playing, one way is to open the level editor ('+' button)
As for 3 and 4, you are 100% right, and it's planned for a future update. In the meantime for the music, you can lower the volume of your headset if needed.

reviews.review_title: Needs some work and stronger curation
reviews.review_description: It took me awhile to figure out how to even open the main menu to start a puzzle. I tried the pancake puzzle and... I guess I'm confused. These puzzles seem like they've been curated a bit but wow who would think it's fun to have 3d puzzle pieces that are itty bitty shavings from the original model that require you to clip your head into the object in order to find the missing inner vertices. Maybe the other puzzles are better and if they are I'd highly recommend advertising them when the game first launches so first impressions are better.

developer_response.body:
Update: starting in version 8, there's now a one-button campaign to guide players through a series of increasingly difficult puzzles. 
Hi! And thanks for taking the time to give feedback! 
You are right that the menu is basic at the moment. Improvements are coming! (including showcasing the best puzzles for new players)
As for the pancake puzzle, the strangest thing is that we had a new player praising the Pancake puzzle just yesterday in our Discord… All puzzles are different, some are very simple with 7 pieces, other are much harder with 120, 150 or 180 pieces… and they are cut by different persons each with their own style. You can even cut your own puzzles.
I'd love if you tried another puzzle, maybe Lighthouse in the sky or Jiggly watermelon jello or one with a lot of pieces (your progress is automatically saved, so you can do it in several sessions) and tell us your impression of these other puzzles. You can chat with other players and the team on Discord: https://discord.gg/CRCbFdFNs8

reviews.review_description: This is a great puzzle game but I have one issue with this game. I've Experienced on three separate puzzles where I know for a fact I completed the whole thing but it still shows I have one piece left but it's no where in sight? Other than that the puzzles are fun looking forward to more.

developer_response.body:
[Update: this is fixed and improved!]
Hi! Thanks for the feedback. If you have 1 on your hand, it means that you have finished as the whole puzzle is considered as one single piece.
You are not the first one to be confused by the 1 in the end, and we have a few improvements coming in the next release, including an end animation that will play once when you finish the puzzle.
If you want to chat with us or other players, you can join our discord (the link to join is on our official site here https://pecopecogame.com/ )

reviews.review_description:
First, thank you developers team of this challenging VR  puzzle.   Please continue to improve the user interface ( user friendly) and allow more options or help funtions.  
Allow to choose the puzzle  level of difficulty ( 20 pieces easy , 50 pieces normal, 80 pieces hard,  100 pieces expert) .
help function to place the piece automatically if you need help .
Option to resize zoom function ( bigger or smaller) the 3D model before the cut begins.   Because bigger 3D model is easier to cut.  Able to have a fined cut.
Limited the number of cut ( 20 cuts for easy , 50 cuts for normal , 80 cuts for hard, 100 cuts expert).
One more suggestion,  Allow to import and export user 3D model of the puzzle into the game if possible .  So player can create customized puzzles.   More colorful and bigger 3D models are fun and challenging to play with.  Please continue to add more puzzle for players to enjoy.  Thank you.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks a lot for all the feedback! We have a new menu system planned, and it will make it much easier to choose your level of difficulty, see the puzzle you already completed, etc.
If you like to cut your own puzzle, we are also planning a way to share them with the community!
If you want to chat with other players, other puzzle cutters and share the puzzle you cut, you can join the Discord: https://discord.gg/CRCbFdFNs8

reviews.review_description: I don't normally like puzzles but this game changed my mind... I just had to know what it looked like in the end!! Great game, lots of potential, can't wait till the devs finish the polish.

It's simple and fun, the movements are smooth, and there are many people to cooperate with, and the price is very reasonable.  Hope to enhance Quest 3's enhanced rendering.

It’s definitely a gem, please keep developing it, it will definitely be a 3A masterpiece in the future.

reviews.review_title: Please continue to update the version
reviews.review_description: Such a great masterpiece and a multiplayer game. As long as it continues to be updated, we will wait and see for better gaming effects. Quest 3 should be even better.  Thanks to the developers for their efforts.

reviews.review_title: The music spoils it... waste of money
reviews.review_description:
Puzzling places is better as no music just really good sound effects...

Please add an option to turn off the rubbish music, i have to turn down the volume otherwise n it spoils it (its the same fckin song on every level!!!!!!!, who wants to play a game with no sound??? I paid for this game n have played it once n music put me off ad rather pay more for puzzling places... (have a go at that game n take notes please developers of this game, there is a demo on app lab db)

This has a lot of potential, but right now it's pretty rough. Love the art style and the quirky models, but the UI is really plain with no feedback or celebration of any sort for finishing a puzzle. Most importantly, it's hard for us to reach half the pieces with how high and low some of them are, and even then it can sometimes get REALLY crowded and confusing fast when you're stuck in one place. Please give us some way to "pull" items to us, or at least some sort of artificial movement to mitigate this.

developer_response.body: [Fixed] we have added distant grab and dedicated animations when you finish a puzzle, so these issues are now solved!

reviews.review_description: Solid game. Relaxing and the puzzles are well done!

I enjoy puzzle games, I find them deeply relaxing with their “go at your own pace” assemble a model in a beautiful outdoor setting vibe. It is a great contrast to action-heavy VR titles (which I also enjoy, but at different times). This game makes assembling virtual 3D models a ton of fun. Currently, there is more than 17 hours of puzzle-assembling content to enjoy (and there is more on the way). It is a game that I can introduce to my mother-in-law who loves puzzles but may not care for VR. Peco Peco is a great addition to anyone’s VR library. Highly recommended!
